title: paepalanthus neopulvinatus
intro: Paepalanthus neopulvinatus, also known as the "Brazilian tree heath," is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Paepalanthus neopulvinatus include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Paepalanthus neopulvinatus can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is believed to provide relief from various health complaints.
